The Way Too Early Ultimatum (Completed 05/07/18)

Page 1 of 2
Speaker 1: All right, welcome back to theme plate three. This is the way too early ultimatum, and
sometimes the best way to get a commitment is for your prospect to see that they're
not committed to the opposite. Now let me say that again, sometimes the best way to
get commitment is for the prospect to see they're not committed to the opposite. So by
leading with a counterintuitive invitation to exclude themselves, to say, "I don't want
this." You get prospects to realize that they're in fact committed to the result and your
prelaunch, that delivers it. So that is what we are going to work on in the third and final
invite of your webinar or launch funnel or whatever type of launch you're using.
And this is really what I would call pulse point number one. It's really kind of where you
check in as a conversion coach and just ask them, based on what you've heard, you've
seen a few messages, are you feeling it? Like is this something you want to continue
talking about? And you get that buy-in. And sometimes when people are faced with that
realty, where like okay now, this person's serious, they're giving me an out right now,
and if they don't take that out, that really ramps up that consistency principle. That's
what really kind of shines that light back on them that, oh wow, okay, now I really do
want to be here. I really do want to do this. It's one of those it's getting real kind of
moments and as a coach who cares about your prospect and the outcomes they want to
achieve, and being willing to challenge them to go after it, this is a very important email
to include.
So this is the way too early ultimatum, it's going to breakdown like this. Since they've
already seen your previous emails, you're just going to start off right away with a call to
action with a little bit of urgency, and then come out with a big outcome, the launch is
promising an invitation to opt-out. This is the big one that a lot of marketers will get
scared of. Why would I put up an invitation to opt-out, well, you know what? That
would actually be a lot more telling than someone just ignoring and not taking actions.
Just putting that in front of them, give them that invitation to opt-out and frame
inaction, frame that inaction if they didn't opt-out, you could frame that lack of opting-
out as implied belongingness.
Like if they didn't opt-out, it's because they should be here. They've decided to be here.
Reflect that back with your second call to action. Close it with that exclusive
empowerment once again, and that optional PS to sell the event launch video, which at
this point, you should already have the content for that based on your other theme
plates. That's it. The big point here is really the invitation for the opt-out. If they're still
there after that, you frame that inaction as implied belongingness. You frame that
choice not to opt-out as a choice to participate, so that's what we're doing.
Let's take a quick look at how this plays out, so example, should I cross you off the list?
That has a little bit of loss aversion in it, right? And curiosity. And we open, hey, we're
kicking things off tomorrow. That's urgency, legitimate urgency for the first installment
of our Profit and Launch workshop series. Just wanted to warn you that I'm going to be
in your inbox like a lot, mostly talking about online courses, product launches, evergreen
funnels, and how to write the kind of killer copy that amplifies the profits in all of them.
So there's that promise, that if that's not something you can get behind, for reals, why
